title: Isidorea pungens
taxonomy: Isidorea pungens is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Isidorea pungens is native to Central America and is a member of the genus Isidorea, which is a group of flowering plants found primarily in tropical regions. The classification of Isidorea pungens is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Gentianales, family Rubiaceae, genus Isidorea, species I. pungens. Further research may be needed to determine the specific classification of this species.

intro: Isidorea pungens, also known as "cacao de montaña," is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, native to Central and South America. Its leaves and bark have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as antidiarrheal, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Isidorea pungens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea or used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal formulations to support digestive health and immune function.
